architecture: CharCNN dual-encoder with InfoNCE contrastive loss

evaluation_gate3_lemma_novelty.note: 11/14 theorems use only 'simp' tactic (no specific named lemma), limiting evaluation coverage. Only 3 theorems had extractable named lemmas.

evaluation_gate3_v2.note: 35/64 theorems had no extractable named lemmas (tactic-only or hypothesis-only proofs). Of the 29 evaluated, most correct lemmas ranked very low (thousands).

conclusion: The contrastive CharCNN dual-encoder with in-batch InfoNCE (batch_size=256) is insufficient for 70K-candidate lemma retrieval. MRR of 0.08-0.33 is far below the 0.786 baseline. Root cause: in-batch negatives (256) cannot provide enough signal to discriminate among 70K lemmas. Next steps: (a) use MoCo-style memory bank with 4096+ negatives, (b) increase batch size to 1024+, (c) use pre-trained LM embeddings instead of char-CNN, or (d) add hard negative mining.
